Structural pauperism and the almshouses

Authors
Affiliations
Department of Economics, Hunter College and The Graduate Center, City University of New York, New York, NY
Department of Economics, Hunter College and The Graduate Center, City University of New York, New York, NY

We are trying to sort out a model in which several causes are building up in a particular way. These causes are:

  1. We have data on the foreign/native composition of the poor in the almshouses in each county in New York State

  2. We know flows - the number of people who enter and leave the almshouse during a year

  3. Questions that concern us: A. Do homeless people tend to leave homelessess when care is better or when it is worse? B. How easily do people substitute between homelessness and work? C. How easily do people move across locations? These questions are interrelated, so maybe our model should suggest this.

  4. Lots of people left New York during this time period, and lots came in from other states, while at the same time an enormous number came from overseas. Can we leverage entry and exit from our problem as ways of normalizing?

Basic utility¶

Agent type¶

To build a structural model, we will start by imagining that there is a type-dependent utility to being homeless and to working in a particular area. We will subscript each type by aaa, where aaa can be some combination of male/female and foreign/native. That’s all we have in the data, so we will go with that.

Location¶

Each location is indexed by a county ccc, generically, where someone starts from influences utility, so we need to carry around a ccc subscript on all of our quantities of interest. Locations offer utility to workers wcw_cwc​, and benefits to being homeless which are hch_chc​. These benefits can depend upon agent type, so we might write wacw_{ac}wac​ and bacb_{ac}bac​ instead. Ultimately, we should extend this even further to include time, so the worker benefits and the homeless benefits are wactw_{act}wact​, and bactb_{act}bact​, but we are going to try and start with a simple model where we don’t think about such things right off the bat.

Moving costs¶

These costs are generally given by mc1c2m_{c_1c_2}mc1​c2​​ - the costs of moving from one county to another, which we will suppose are symmetric for now so we have something like a distance matrix with 0 on the diagonal. But they might also depend upon whether or not you are homeless, and also your current type. Hence, we should write mc1c2asm^{as}_{c_1c_2}mc1​c2​as​, where s=w,hs=w,hs=w,h are possible states.

Inclusive utilities¶

There are various ways to nest things, and one way is to nest within a location. We might do this if we expect that someone chooses a location first, and then chooses whether to work or be homeless there. How might we describe this situation? Given all of our above notations, we have (still leaving out time subscripts):

Uacicj=λln⁡[exp⁡(wacj−mcicjasλ)+exp⁡(hacj−mcicjasλ)]U_{ac_ic_j}=\lambda \ln \left[\exp\left(\frac{w_{ac_j}-m^{as}_{c_ic_j}}{\lambda}\right)+\exp\left(\frac{h_{ac_j}-m^{as}_{c_ic_j}}{\lambda}\right)\right]Uaci​cj​​=λln[exp(λwacj​​−mci​cj​as​​)+exp(λhacj​​−mci​cj​as​​)]
(1)

This describes the utility that an agent who is currently in location cic_ici​ would get if they were to move to cjc_jcj​. Mind you, the agent does not yet know if he or she will be homeless or working in location cjc_jcj​, so the agent gets the above expected utility from moving to cjc_jcj​ at some cost. When there, to complete the story, the agent draws a frechet random variable upon arrival, and then decides, based on this idiosyncratic payoff component, to be homeless or to work. Note that this inclusive utility simplifies to:

Uacicj=λln⁡[exp⁡(wacjλ)+exp⁡(hacjλ)]−mcicjasU_{ac_ic_j}=\lambda \ln \left[\exp\left(\frac{w_{ac_j}}{\lambda}\right)+\exp\left(\frac{h_{ac_j}}{\lambda}\right)\right]-m^{as}_{c_ic_j}Uaci​cj​​=λln[exp(λwacj​​​)+exp(λhacj​​​)]−mci​cj​as​
(2)

The great thing about inclusive utilities is they give probabilities through something like Roy’s Identity. So, we can get the probability that someone moving from iii to jjj works in location jjj is the derivative of the above utility with respect to wacjw_{ac_j}wacj​​:

∂Uacicj∂wacj=exp⁡(wacjλ)exp⁡(wacjλ)+exp⁡(hacjλ)=Pw,acj\frac{\partial U_{ac_ic_j}}{\partial w_{ac_j}}=\frac{\exp\left(\frac{w_{ac_j}}{\lambda}\right)}{\exp\left(\frac{w_{ac_j}}{\lambda}\right)+\exp\left(\frac{h_{ac_j}}{\lambda}\right)}=P_{w,acj}∂wacj​​∂Uaci​cj​​​=exp(λwacj​​​)+exp(λhacj​​​)exp(λwacj​​​)​=Pw,acj​
(3)

Note that because of the way in which moving costs linearize out of the utility they do not influence the decision to work or not in the new location. Also, we note that the decision to work or not doesn’t depend upon the origin.

Each agent at the beginning of every period chooses where to go to, and we can write the utility of a type aaa agent in location cic_ici​ as:

Uaci=μln⁡[∑cj=1Cexp⁡(Uacicjμ)]\mathbf{U}_{ac_i} = \mu\ln\left[\sum_{c_j=1}^C \exp\left(\frac{U_{ac_ic_j}}{\mu}\right)\right]Uaci​​=μln⎣⎡​cj​=1∑C​exp(μUaci​cj​​​)⎦⎤​
(4)

Where UacicjU_{ac_ic_j}Uaci​cj​​ is as above is expected utility.

Entry and Exit¶

Now, we suppose that agents can enter or exit the game. Let’s not worry too much about how all this goes for now, but suppose that there is no real nesting here, and agents get utility eXcie^{X_{c_i}}eXci​​ from leaving if they are currently in location cic_ici​. Inclusive utility is then given by

Uaci=ln⁡[exp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)]\mathcal{U}_{ac_i}=\ln \left[\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf{U_{ac_i}})\right]Uaci​​=ln[exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)]
(5)

This expression can be used to derive the probability that an agent stays in state. For example, we find that a fraction of the inhabitants PxciP_{xc_i}Pxci​​

Pxci=eXcieXci+eUaciP_{xc_i} = \frac{e^{X_{c_i}}}{e^{X_{c_i}}+e^{\mathbf{U}_{ac_i}}}Pxci​​=eXci​​+eUaci​​eXci​​​
(6)

leave.

Some results¶

Now, using the above notation, we can derive conditional probabilities as follows. So, the probability that an individual from cjc_jcj​ stays in New York, goes to county cic_ici​, and then works, is:

P(cj,work∣ci)=exp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)⋅exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)⋅exp⁡(wacj/λ)exp⁡(wacj/λ)+exp⁡(hacj/λ).P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} {\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} \cdot \frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)} \cdot \frac{\exp\left(w_{ac_j}/\lambda\right)} {\exp\left(w_{ac_j}/\lambda\right)+\exp\left(h_{ac_j}/\lambda\right)}.P(cj​,work∣ci​)=exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)exp(Uaci​​)​⋅∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​⋅exp(wacj​​/λ)+exp(hacj​​/λ)exp(wacj​​/λ)​.
(7)

Maybe it is more informative to write it in terms of the primitive object UacicjU_{ac_ic_j}Uaci​cj​​:

P(cj,work∣ci)=[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)]μexp⁡(Xci)+[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)]μ⋅exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)⋅exp⁡(wacj/λ)exp⁡(wacj/λ)+exp⁡(hacj/λ).P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) = \frac{ \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} }{ \exp(X_{c_i}) + \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} } \cdot \frac{ \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right) }{ \sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right) } \cdot \frac{ \exp\left(w_{ac_j}/\lambda\right) }{ \exp\left(w_{ac_j}/\lambda\right) + \exp\left(h_{ac_j}/\lambda\right) }.P(cj​,work∣ci​)=exp(Xci​​)+[∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)]μ[∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)]μ​⋅∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​⋅exp(wacj​​/λ)+exp(hacj​​/λ)exp(wacj​​/λ)​.
(8)

Maybe even:

P(cj,work∣ci)=[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)]μ−1exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)exp⁡(Xci)+[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)]μ⋅exp⁡(wacj/λ)exp⁡(Uacicj+mcicjasλ).P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) = \frac{ \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu-1} \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right) }{ \exp(X_{c_i}) + \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} } \cdot \frac{ \exp\left(w_{ac_j}/\lambda\right) }{ \exp\left( \frac{U_{ac_i c_j}+m^{as}_{c_i c_j}}{\lambda} \right) }.P(cj​,work∣ci​)=exp(Xci​​)+[∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)]μ[∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)]μ−1exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​⋅exp(λUaci​cj​​+mci​cj​as​​)exp(wacj​​/λ)​.
(9)

Now, if we wanted to, we could let X→∞X\rightarrow \inftyX→∞ in which case we would have a closed system, and we can see how the μ\muμ term kind of drops out from aspects of the converation. Anyways, let’s forget about the subscripts aaa and sss for now, and suppose all agents are identical just to see how things are going to aggregate and what we can make of this.

Here is a simplified expression:

P(cj,work∣ci)=[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μ−1exp⁡(Ucicj/μ)exp⁡(Xci)+[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μ⋅exp⁡(wcj/λ)exp⁡(Ucicj+mcicjλNci).P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) = \frac{ \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu-1} \exp\left(U_{c_i c_j}/\mu\right) }{ \exp(X_{c_i}) + \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} } \cdot \frac{ \exp\left(w_{c_j}/\lambda\right) }{ \exp\left( \frac{U_{c_i c_j}+m_{c_i c_j}}{\lambda}N_{c_i} \right) }.P(cj​,work∣ci​)=exp(Xci​​)+[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ−1exp(Uci​cj​​/μ)​⋅exp(λUci​cj​​+mci​cj​​​Nci​​)exp(wcj​​/λ)​.
(10)

Now, suppose we have a vector of starting populations in each location as NciN_{c_i}Nci​​. We can then compute next period populations as Ncj′N'_{c_j}Ncj​′​, say, as follows; this is actually next period’s working population.

Ncj′=∑ci=1CNciP(cj,work∣ci)=∑ci=1CNci[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μ−1exp⁡(Ucicj/μ)exp⁡(Xci)+[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μ⋅exp⁡(wcj/λ)exp⁡(Ucicj+mcicjλ).N'_{c_j} = \sum_{{c_i}=1}^C N_{c_i}P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) =\sum_{{c_i}=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{ \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu-1} \exp\left(U_{c_i c_j}/\mu\right) }{ \exp(X_{c_i}) + \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} } \cdot \frac{ \exp\left(w_{c_j}/\lambda\right) }{ \exp\left( \frac{U_{c_i c_j}+m_{c_i c_j}}{\lambda} \right) }.Ncj​′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​P(cj​,work∣ci​)=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ−1exp(Uci​cj​​/μ)​⋅exp(λUci​cj​​+mci​cj​​​)exp(wcj​​/λ)​.
(11)

The population is just:

Ncj′=∑ci=1CNciP(cj,work∣ci)=∑ci=1CNci[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μ−1exp⁡(Ucicj/μ)exp⁡(Xci)+[∑k=1Cexp⁡(Ucick/μ)]μN'_{c_j} = \sum_{{c_i}=1}^C N_{c_i}P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) =\sum_{{c_i}=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{ \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu-1} \exp\left(U_{c_i c_j}/\mu\right) }{ \exp(X_{c_i}) + \left[\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{c_i c_k}/\mu\right)\right]^{\mu} }Ncj​′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​P(cj​,work∣ci​)=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ[∑k=1C​exp(Uci​ck​​/μ)]μ−1exp(Uci​cj​​/μ)​
(12)

We also might note from this that we have a substantial number of people who leave the system. Out migrants are given by:

NX′=∑ci=1CNciexp⁡Xciexp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)N'_X = \sum_{c_i=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{\exp{X_{c_i}}}{\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf{U_{ac_i}})}NX′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)expXci​​​
(13)

We might also get an expression for the number of incoming people as something like:

NI=S(exp⁡(Uaci))N_I = S(\exp(\mathbf{U_{ac_i}}))NI​=S(exp(Uaci​​))
(14)

In fact, maybe something is taking shape here. Let’s movers from cic_ici​ to cjc_jcj​ as follows:

P(cj,work∣ci)=exp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)⋅exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)P(c_j,\text{work}\mid c_i) = \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} {\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} \cdot \frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)}P(cj​,work∣ci​)=exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)exp(Uaci​​)​⋅∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​
(15)

Then, we get population as:

Ncj′=∑ci=1CNciexp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)⋅exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)N'_{c_j} = \sum_{c_i=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} {\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} \cdot \frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)}Ncj​′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)exp(Uaci​​)​⋅∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​
(16)

People leaving the system entirely are:

NX′=∑ci=1CNciexp⁡Xciexp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)N'_X = \sum_{c_i=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{\exp{X_{c_i}}}{\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf{U_{ac_i}})}NX′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)expXci​​​
(17)

But we also have people who enter the system. These are given by:

NI=Wexp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Uaci)+exp⁡(Y)N_I = W \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})}{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})+\exp(Y)}NI​=Wexp(Uaci​​)+exp(Y)exp(Uaci​​)​
(18)

So the number of in-migrants who arrive in place cjc_jcj​ are:

NIcj=Wexp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Uaci)+exp⁡(Y)exp⁡(Uac1cj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uac1ck/μ)N_{Ic_j} = W \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})}{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})+\exp(Y)}\frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_1 c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_1 c_k}/\mu\right)}NIcj​​=Wexp(Uaci​​)+exp(Y)exp(Uaci​​)​∑k=1C​exp(Uac1​ck​​/μ)exp(Uac1​cj​​/μ)​
(19)

Giving us the total population in place NcjN_{c_j}Ncj​​ as:

Ncj′=∑ci=1CNciexp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Xci)+exp⁡(Uaci)⋅exp⁡(Uacicj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uacick/μ)+Wexp⁡(Uaci)exp⁡(Uaci)+exp⁡(Y)exp⁡(Uac1cj/μ)∑k=1Cexp⁡(Uac1ck/μ)N'_{c_j} = \sum_{c_i=1}^C N_{c_i} \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} {\exp(X_{c_i})+\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})} \cdot \frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_i c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_i c_k}/\mu\right)}+W \frac{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})}{\exp(\mathbf U_{ac_i})+\exp(Y)}\frac{\exp\left(U_{ac_1 c_j}/\mu\right)} {\sum_{k=1}^C \exp\left(U_{ac_1 c_k}/\mu\right)}Ncj​′​=ci​=1∑C​Nci​​exp(Xci​​)+exp(Uaci​​)exp(Uaci​​)​⋅∑k=1C​exp(Uaci​ck​​/μ)exp(Uaci​cj​​/μ)​+Wexp(Uaci​​)+exp(Y)exp(Uaci​​)​∑k=1C​exp(Uac1​ck​​/μ)exp(Uac1​cj​​/μ)​
(20)
